title: jacea phrygia
side_effects: Jacea phrygia can have side effects if used improperly, such as excessive consumption leading to stomach discomfort, nausea, and diarrhea. In some cases, prolonged use of the plant can cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Additionally, improper preparation or dosage of Jacea phrygia may also lead to interactions with other medications, resulting in adverse effects such as increased heart rate or blood pressure. Furthermore, using Jacea phrygia without proper knowledge or guidance can also cause liver damage or kidney failure in severe cases.
